subject: Physics
title: Thomas Edison: The Inventor Factory
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Thomas Edison: The Inventor Factory.

hook.text: Edison didn't just invent the light bulb. He built the first power station, the phonograph, and a movie camera.

buildup.text: His Menlo Park lab was the world's first industrial research laboratory—invention became a business.

discovery.text: Edison tested thousands of materials for the light bulb filament. He famously said he found 10,000 ways that didn't work.

twist.text: In 1882, he lit up a square mile of Manhattan from his Pearl Street Station—the first commercial power grid.

climax.text: Edison held 1,093 US patents—the most by any individual for over a century.

punchline.text: 1,093 patents. The light bulb. The power grid. Invention industrialized.

quiz.question: What was historic about Edison's Pearl Street Station?
options: It was the first commercial electrical power distribution system
It was the first building lit by candles
